_doc: License allowlist for the FRONTEND tree, mirroring the backend tomte/configs/tox.ini [Licenses] block (liccheck PARANOID posture). Every PRODUCTION dependency's license must match `allowedSpdx`, be resolved by `licenseOverrides`, or be exempted (by exact NAME, or by narrow PREFIX for publisher-controlled platform-binary families). Unknown or unlisted licenses FAIL — they are resolved, not silenced. See frontend/scripts/license-check.mjs and CONTRIBUTING.md ('Supply-chain checks').
_keep_when_empty: Do NOT delete this file when overrides/exemptions are empty. Empty arrays are the running assertion of 'license gate exists AND zero current debt' — pre-paves the schema, preserves git history of past resolutions, keeps the CODEOWNERS-routed surface, and lets cross-repo tooling distinguish 'clean gate' (count=0) from 'not in use' (no file).

_fields.scope: 'production' (default) or 'all'. 'production' mirrors the backend liccheck running against `tomte freeze-dependencies` — devDeps don't ship and generate considerable transitive-license noise.
_fields.allowedSpdx: SPDX identifiers (plus npm-ecosystem aliases like 'Apache2', 'Apache 2.0') that pass the gate. Mirror tomte [Licenses] authorized_licenses verbatim where it applies to npm. Additions beyond tomte (e.g. OFL-1.1 for @fontsource fonts, Artistic-2.0 for transitive dev tooling) are explicit extensions.
_fields.unauthorizedSpdx: SPDX identifiers that ALWAYS fail, regardless of override or exemption. Mirror tomte unauthorized_licenses verbatim. Default-deny via PARANOID covers anything not on either list; this array is reserved for licenses we want to call out by name.
_fields.licenseOverrides: Package whose declared license is wrong (UNKNOWN, malformed, or the package.json field disagrees with the LICENSE file). Each entry RESOLVES the license to a specific SPDX value — these are CORRECTIONS, not policy exceptions. Never used to silence a real copyleft. Fields: package (exact name), spdx (resolved SPDX), reason, added (YYYY-MM-DD), review (YYYY-MM-DD).
_fields.exemptions: Packages allowed despite their license being on `unauthorizedSpdx` or otherwise unresolved. Exact NAME match, version-agnostic. Fields: package (exact name), spdx, reason, added (YYYY-MM-DD), review (YYYY-MM-DD).
_fields.exemptionPrefixes: Narrow prefix exemptions for publisher-controlled npm scopes with a uniform license stance (e.g. @img/sharp-libvips-* platform binaries, all LGPL-3.0-or-later, all published by the sharp team). NEVER use for general namespaces like @types/, where each sub-package is independently licensed by its author. Each entry needs the same justification fields as `exemptions[]`, keyed by `prefix` instead of `package`.

exemptionPrefixes.reason: Platform-specific libvips native binaries that Next.js next/image loads at request time (dynamically linked, not statically combined with our JS). Package name varies by OS/arch (linux-x64, darwin-arm64, etc.), so an exact-name list would be non-deterministic across CI and local dev. `@img` is a publisher-controlled npm scope with a uniform license stance (the sharp team's native binaries), which is the only situation where a prefix exemption is appropriate.
